name: Amish-Style Chicken and Corn Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the stewing hen with chicken stock, coarsely chopped onions, carrots, celery, and saffron threads in a large pot.
2. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
3. Simmer for about 1 hour, skimming any foam or impurities from the surface as needed.
4. Remove the stewing hen and let it cool enough to handle before picking out the meat from the bones. Cut into pieces.
5. Strain the broth through a fine sieve and discard the solids. (Note: The soup can be prepared up to this point in advance, refrigerated or frozen for 2-3 days. Label containers with date when reheating.)
6. Add corn, celery, parsley, and cooked noodles to the pot.
7. Return the broth to a simmer over medium heat.
8. Serve immediately.
